title: geum macrophyllum subsp. perincisum
intro: Geum macrophyllum subsp. perincisum, also known as the large-flowered avens or Perincisum avens, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Geum macrophyllum subsp. perincisum is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used to treat a variety of health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Geum macrophyllum subsp. perincisum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
